missables: Sun Crest — on the floor after the Yunalesca fight. Do NOT leave without picking it up — it's Tidus's Caladbolg upgrade prerequisite.
sgTip: Yunalesca removes all positive statuses at the start of each phase. Don't pre-buff — wait until after her opener. Zombie protection (Zombie Ward or Deathproof armor) is critical for Phase 3.

subLocations.name: Zanarkand Dome Approach
subLocations.prose: After camp at the familiar hill, the party finally enters Zanarkand Ruins. The early maps are linear and guarded by the same fiends from Gagazet. Don't miss the Fortune Sphere and Spiritual Targe on the approach — they're in chests off the main path. Inside the dome, Defender Z fiends hit hard; use Armor Break and Mental Break to manage them. Defender Z are also one of the best sources of Lv. 4 Key Spheres in the game — steal or bribe them if your Sphere Grid needs unlocking late-gate nodes.

subLocations.name: Zanarkand Dome Interior
subLocations.prose: The dome interior holds several more chests including a Friend Sphere and rare Lv. 3 Key Sphere — check the eastern platforms and the debris paths carefully. Braska's story unfolds here before Yunalesca. After the fight, descend the broken staircase behind her and pick up the Sun Crest from the chest — it's easy to walk out without it. The Destruction Sphere chest in the Zanarkand Cloister can only be retrieved after you return via airship.

items.name: Sun Crest (Tidus's Caladbolg — pick up AFTER Yunalesca)
Magistral Rod (Destruction Sphere — return via airship, required for Anima)

bosses.strategy: HP: 52,000. No elemental weakness. Steal: Ether / Turbo Ether. The fight takes place on six platforms arranged in a circle around the boss. Spectral Keeper's Tera-Gravitation places a detonation marker on the platform of the targeted character — that platform explodes at the end of the turn. Move any marked character to an unoccupied platform immediately or they die. Spread the party across non-adjacent platforms from the start; counterattacks hit the three platforms centred on the attacker, so keeping characters one platform apart prevents a single counter from wiping multiple party members. Prioritise keeping Yuna on a safe platform at all times. Glyph Mines appear on random platforms and also explode after one turn — treat them the same way. Berserk (from the tail attack) is dangerous because berserked characters won't dodge mines automatically; Esuna or a Remedy clears it. Apply Mental Break to amplify Lulu's magic damage, then grind down HP with your strongest damage dealers.

Three forms: humanoid (24k HP) → lamia (48k HP) → full undead (60k HP). In the first form Yunalesca counters physical attacks with Darkness, spells with Silence, and special techniques with Sleep — equip armor that resists her counter, or use Reflect to deflect her responses. In the second form she adds Hellbiter, which inflicts Zombie; don't be too aggressive about curing it since you need at least one Zombie character active in the final form. In the third form her Mega-Death instantly kills every non-Zombie character — keep at least one or two characters Zombied at all times going into this phase and cure any active Regen with Dispel (Regen will heal a Zombie character to death). Cast Haste and Protect on the party before she reaches full power, then bring in your strongest aeons and unload Overdrives.
